hook.why_reader_cares: an investor reading this is deciding where the next check goes in the agent stack; the hook has to make the money question feel urgent and contrarian

introduction: context: 2026 framed everywhere as the year agents 'go to work'
problem: most of what investors see is demos; budgets and moats are the real signal
what_this_covers: where the money is, who is winning, what is still vaporware, and which layer to underwrite

sections.title: From Demo to Budget Line
key_points: the spend is real and concentrated

sections.title: Follow the Money, Not the Models
key_points: funding tilting to orchestration vs raw model bets
token prices collapsing → margin moves up the stack

sections.title: Reliability Is the Whole Game
key_points: capability is not the constraint; multi-step success is
a named production proof-point with a number

sections.title: Will Agents Eat the SaaS Seat?
The Investable Layer
key_points: moat sits in orchestration / eval / integration
what to underwrite and what to avoid

conclusion.summary: agents are real, the money is moving up the stack, and reliability is the moat
conclusion.call_to_action: underwrite the orchestration-and-reliability layer; demand task-completion metrics from founders

source: content-research-writer step 2 — Collaborative Outlining (Hook / Introduction / Main Sections + research-needed markers / Conclusion)
